attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches from 250m out, often from 5-7 positions back. Excels on technical finishes with late turns or geometry-based positioning advantages rather than pure power straights.
team_tactics: Historically dependent on structured leadout trains (Lotto-Soudal 2019-2020), but showed positioning independence in 2023. Team domestique shortage at BikeExchange/Jayco exposed vulnerability when leadout dissolves or on transitional stages.
terrain_preference: Technical sprint finishes with roundabouts, late turns, or rising/cobbled sections where positioning and timing trump pure watts. Severely exposed on anything above 3-4% gradients or transitional stages with late climbs.
vulnerability: Crashes frequently in sprint positioning (5 documented incidents). Cannot follow on climbs above 4%, gets isolated and time-cut threatened. Completely reliant on structured approaches when leadout disrupted—checks sprint if chaos occurs.
key_patterns: Wins from deep in field (7+ positions back) by overhauling organized leadout trains. Documented 3 times.
Crashes/mechanicals in critical moments: 5 separate incidents including wheel contact, tangles, positioning errors.
Dropped and isolated on any gradient above 4%: finished 2+ minutes down multiple times.
Superior race craft on technical finishes with geometry advantages: wins on cobbles, roundabouts, late turns.
Exceptional recovery capacity: crashed inside 10km then won same stage in 2019 Tour.
Team domestique shortage exposed 2022: isolated immediately when attacks occur, broom wagon chased.
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Tour showed return to competitive sprinting (2nd, 3rd) without full leadout support, suggesting positioning maturity after injury-plagued 2021-2022 seasons
